symptoms: Water-soaked spots appear on leaves and increase in size
Spots turn purple-brown and finally black
White fungal growth develops on the underside of leaves
Infection spreads to petioles, rachis, and stems
Stem breaks at nodes causing plant to topple
Tubers show purplish-brown spots and rusty brown internal necrosis
spread_and_survival: Infected tubers and soil act as primary sources of infection
Disease persists through infected tubers across seasons
epidemiology: Rainfall of at least 0.1 mm supports disease spread
management: Spray mancozeb or zineb at 0.2% as preventive measure
Avoid injury to tubers during harvest
Use resistant varieties like Kufri Naveen, Kufri Jeevan, Kufri Alankar, Kufri Khasi Garo, Kufri Moti
Destroy foliage a few days before harvest using herbicide

symptoms: Occurs in both hills and plains
Brown to black necrotic spots on leaves
Spots are angular or oval with concentric rings
Multiple spots merge and spread across the leaf
Shot holes may appear in tubers
spread_and_survival: Fungus survives in soil and plant debris for over 17 months
Primary infection occurs from overwintered mycelium
Secondary spread through wind, rain, and insects
epidemiology: Dry and warm weather with intermittent rain
More common in poorly nourished crops
management: Remove and destroy infected plant debris
Spray Zineb or Captan at 0.2% every 15–20 days
